element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic adjacency rules and turn structure before first battle
Tutorial battles teach unit positioning and movement in turn-based tactics
Wizards introduce resource scarcity decisions (choose wizard or hire alternative unit)
Available immediately but optional; teaches tactical positioning depth

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Tactics screen, available from early turns
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Accessible but requires understanding territorial adjacency first

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Tactics screen, available per territory per turn
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource management layer builds after player understands military conquest
Four scenarios present different family configurations; complexity emerges mid-game
Long-term goal requiring sustained strategy across full game campaign

typical_relief_triggers: Completing upgrade cycle on owned territory
End of turn with no enemy attacks
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy declares attack on adjacent territory
Loss of key unit or wizard in combat
Resource scarcity forcing difficult upgrade choices
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based strategy with discretionary action pacing; player controls tempo during tactics phase but forced into real-time decision-making once battle screen activates

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Diplomatic options and defensive upgrades are optional; pure conquest-focused play is viable

difficulty_curve.early_game: Shallow learning curve for basic conquest; scenarios lock player into starting family configuration and limited initial territories, easing into mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Exponential complexity spike: resource management, multi-family pressure, and alliance betrayal create overlapping decision layers
difficulty_curve.late_game: Overwhelming complexity cited by reviewers; endgame requires balancing offense against multiple entrenched factions while maintaining economic momentum
notable_spikes: Transition from first victory to map-wide conquest when player realizes true scope
Mid-game when 3+ enemy families converge on player territories
Late game resource bottlenecks when remaining families are strongest

notable_level_patterns.description: Four distinct scenarios use identical game board but vary starting family lineups, initial provinces, vassal counts, and gem distribution; each presents different strategic path to victory
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Scenario selection menu (all four variants available from start)

notable_level_patterns.description: Strategic layer (tactics screen, resource management, diplomacy) triggers transition to tactical battle layer when combat initiated; forces different decision-making tempo and spatial awareness
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any player-initiated attack or received attack triggers battle screen

notable_level_patterns.description: Only adjacent territories attackable; forces players to conquer in contiguous chains; creates natural chokepoints and incentivizes forward momentum over scattered territorial grabs
